title: lichen_util.h:54 secure_zero returns early on len==0 without barrier
description: Contract violation: secure_zero() - Returns at line 63 if len==0 without executing compiler_barrier(). This is technically correct (nothing to zero) but inconsistent with the function's security contract. A zero-length call could theoretically be used as a timing-safe marker for 'something was zeroed', and skipping the barrier changes timing. WHY: Inconsistent behavior. HOW to fix: Move compiler_barrier() after len==0 check, or add barrier before early return. Very minor, P3.
